title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: **Minimal canary contract (v1)** - Add one new smoke script: `tests/test-kernel-post-bootstrap-canary.sh` (and wire from gate) that runs **supported launcher path** in a PTY: `codex-prompt-launch vote "CANARY_ID=<uuid>"`. - Record `t0` and `canary_id`; pass only if all checks succeed within `180s`. **Pass conditions (strict, low false-positive)** - Output contains startup ack: `Local consensus mode is active.` - A **new bootstrap evidence file** created after `t0` exists under bootstrap state root, and JSON matches: - `prompt_name` is `vote`/`v` - `bootstrap_task` contains `CANARY_ID=<uuid>` - providers include both `claude` and `glm` with `ok=true` - Keep process alive for a short dwell (`45s`) after ack; fail if it emits `quiescence_timeout` or approval-prompt text. **Why this is minimal and robust** - Uses existing runtime artifacts (no broad redesign): bootstrap evidence path/root already exists and is session-localizable by timestamp + token. [launcher bootstrap state root](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L47) [evidence payload fields](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-prompt-launcher/bin/codex-prompt-launch#L218) - Avoids stale/global-log false positives by requiring `CANARY_ID` in current-run evidence. - Avoids non-TTY false negatives by using PTY. - Still fail-closed: any missing provider evidence, missing token, approval prompt, or early instability => fail.
